perturbation.original_text: i.any information concerning the Franchise, even if this information is known or available to the general public;
perturbation.changed_text: i.any information concerning the Franchise that is explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Franchisor in writing;
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the scope of 'Confidential information'. The original definition includes all information concerning the franchise, even if publicly known. The modified definition restricts it to only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Franchisor in writing. This creates a contradiction with the original intent where even known information should be kept confidential. This could be in contradiction with section 2, making the non-disclosure agreement ambigious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The potential Franchisee recognizes that any confidential information could be used to the detriment of the Franchisor and the Franchise, and that the Franchisee disclosing any confidential information could cause irreparable damage to the Franchisor and the Franchise. The potential Franchisee hereby agrees to respect unconditionally the confidential nature of all confidential information and to never disclose or communicate any confidential information to a third party, be it directly or indirectly, or knowingly use this information for any purpose whatsoever, except with the Franchisor’s written consent. Furthermore, the potential Franchisee agrees to not discuss or communicate any aspect whatsoever of the confidential information, be it directly or indirectly, to any agent, director, employee, or any other representative EXCEPT if these individuals (i) have been informed of the confidential nature of the information and (ii) have duly completed and signed the certificate presented in Annex “A”. The potential Franchisee will be held accountable for any violation of this Agreement committed by their directors, agents, employees, representatives, and other professional representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined confidential information broadly, including even publicly available information related to the franchise. The changed text narrows this definition to only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Franchisor in writing. This contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the potential Franchisee to unconditionally respect the confidential nature of 'all confidential information'. The modified definition significantly reduces what constitutes 'confidential information,' potentially exempting information that the contradicted text intends to protect, leading to an ambiguity that causes a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The potential Franchisee agrees to take the necessary measures to ensure that the provisions of the present Agreement are respected to the same or greater degree that they would reasonably expect the confidentiality of their own information of the same nature to be respected.
perturbation.changed_text: The potential Franchisee agrees to take measures to ensure that the provisions of the present Agreement are respected to the degree that the potential Franchisee deems necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes a standard of care for protecting confidential information based on how the potential Franchisee protects their own similar information. The modified text gives the potential Franchisee complete discretion in determining the 'necessary measures.' This contradicts the intent of a strict confidentiality agreement, undermining the Franchisor's protection and creating potential conflicts with Section 5 which grants the Franchisor the right to an injunction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The potential Franchisee agrees that, in the event of a real or possible violation of any provision of the present Agreement by the Franchisee, the Franchisor has the right to seek an injunction in order to stop or prevent any violation of the present Agreement by the potential Franchisee, as well as the right to pursue without restriction any duty, right, or recourse legally available to the Franchisor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the potential Franchisee complete discretion in determining the 'necessary measures' to protect confidential information. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which grants the Franchisor the right to seek an injunction to prevent any violation of the agreement. The change undermines the Franchisor's ability to enforce the agreement and protect its confidential information, as the potential Franchisee could argue that the measures they deemed 'necessary' were insufficient, thus weakening the Franchisor's right to an injunction. This falls under the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type, as the inconsistent definition of the required measures creates uncertainty and conflict between the two sections.

perturbation.original_text: All documents, letters, reports, protocols, notes, journals, drawings, plans, maps, sketches, spreadsheets, data, and any other written material, correspondence, archive, or e-mail (including any electronic material) containing confidential information, as well as any copies of these materials, must be returned by the potential Franchisee upon written request to do so from the Franchisor or at any time at the Franchisor’s discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: All documents created by the potential Franchisee, including letters, reports, and notes derived from the confidential information, must be returned by the potential Franchisee upon written request to do so from the Franchisor, unless the potential franchisee has intellectual property rights in such derivated information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the return of confidential information. If the potential franchisee derives intellectual property from the confidential information, they are not required to return the documents. This creates a contradiction, because, previously there was no exceptions made on the return of confidential information. Now, the potential Franchisee could argue they don't need to return information due to their rights. This could be in contradiction with the definitions outlined previously. Furthermore, creates an uncertainty on the use of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this document, the following terms will have the following definitions: (a)“Third person” includes, but is not limited to, any person, company, association, organization, union, corporation, and any other entity or individual; (b)“Confidential information” refers to and encompasses any information acquired or received by the potential Franchisee from or concerning the Franchisor, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing: i.any information concerning the Franchise, even if this information is known or available to the general public; ii.any information or data concerning the Franchise and the Franchisor; iii.any financial information concerning the Franchise and the Franchisor; iv.the present confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all documents containing confidential information must be returned. The changed text introduces an exception: if the potential franchisee has intellectual property rights in the derived information, they are not required to return the documents. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential information' broadly, encompassing any information acquired from the Franchisor, even if publicly available. This creates a direct conflict. The changed text now allows the franchisee to retain documents containing information that is defined as confidential, contradicting the earlier, all-encompassing definition and return requirement. This falls under the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' type because the definition of confidential information is now ambiguous in its application.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall ensure to the benefit of, and be binding upon, the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall ensure to the benefit of the Franchisor, and be binding upon the parties hereto. The benefits to the potential franchisee only extend if a franchise agreement is signed. This agreement is not automatically transferrable to assignees for the potential franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: This modification makes the benefits of the agreement one-sided, primarily favoring the Franchisor. It introduces a contradiction because the original text suggests mutual benefit and binding obligations for all parties involved, including successors and assigns, this introduces a imbalance in what benefit each party gets, especially given that for the potential franchisee, it only applies if the aggrement is signed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall ensure to the benefit of the Franchisor, and be binding upon the parties hereto. The benefits to the potential franchisee only extend if a franchise agreement is signed. This agreement is not automatically transferrable to assignees for the potential franchisee.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement benefits and binds all parties, including successors and assigns. The changed text restricts the benefits to the Franchisor and limits the franchisee's benefits to only if a franchise agreement is signed, and the agreement is not automatically transferrable to assignees. This contradicts the original intent of mutual benefit and creates an imbalance and restricts transferability, creating a clear contradiction, as well as the provided explanation stating it makes the benefits one sided.
